plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-446 RP14.P07.M02.S16-RP14.P07.M09.S11 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-446 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP14.P07 foundation slice: the M02 Type And Shape Definition tail, the full M03 Primary Implementation Slice, M04 Secondary Workflow Slice, M05 Permission And Audit Binding, M06 Synthetic Fixture Set, M07 Test And Golden Case Set, and M08 Hermes Evidence Packet cycles, and the M09 Claude Review Packet head as eight descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H14), Claude review packet (C14), and CP00-447 handoff, without opening settlement, origination, allocation, or working credit runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-446 (RP14.P07.M02.S16-RP14.P07.M09.S11, risk C, 150 units) is a descriptor-only settlement-core closeout pack. Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claim is appropriately conservative and fully supported: manifest declares production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, status=implemented_pending_review. Independently verified the 150-unit deliverable distribution (failure_recovery 82, security_audit 14, test 14, implementation 13, hermes_evidence 13, claude_review 7, fixture 7) matches the prompt, manifest delivery_counts, and registry deliverable_counts. Source inspection of service.js, validators.js, and registry.js shows pure frozen-object descriptor construction with no real IO/network/DB/runtime (grep for writeFile/fetch/child_process/fs/http/SQL returned only boolean attestation flags). Descriptor-only guards (descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, writes_product_state=false, loads_real_fixture_data=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, exposes_raw_payload=false) are enforced in code, validated by validators, and asserted by tests. Authority boundary holds end-to-end: claude_is_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required=true. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence packets/receipts, never product runtime. Review hardening forbidden_review_evidence covers all CP178+ rejection cases and the auth preflight passed with valid JSON. CP00-446 is consistent across the live closeout-pack-plan.json, the settlement contract current_pack/no_write_attestation, and the registry binding; all 7 required public exports exist and are tested; recorded validation commands all returned exit 0. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) and a valid review receipt are correctly still pending and remain required before commit/closeout — this is by design and already enforced by the manifest status and hardening sequence. No P0/P1/P2/P3 defects found; Claude remains a read-only reviewer and not the final approval authority.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-446 settlement core p07 foundation slice descriptor pack
